figures.caption: Figure 3: Synthetic 3D cyclone-wind outlier-identification example using sparse quiver rendering. (a) Observed vector field with injected anomalies (red arrows). (b) Local-mean vector field from normalized convolution. (c) Detected outliers at k = 3.0, with true positives (red), false positives (orange), and missed injected points (blue). Top row: panels (a) and (b); bottom row: panel (c).
Figure 4: Threshold sweep for 3D cyclone-wind outlier identification using |∥v∥ − µ∥v∥| > kσ∥v∥, reporting precision, recall, and F1 (mean ± std across seeds).

Figure 5: Real-radar polar demonstration on uncorrected DOW6 X-band reflectivity from WINTRE-MIX (2022-02-23), with shared color scale. Panels (a), (b), and (c) show the full-domain raw field, W = 5 smoothing, and W = 15 smoothing. Panels (d), (e), and (f) show the corresponding 20 km × 20 km central zoom. The comparison highlights mixed polar boundaries (periodic azimuth, reflective range), robust behavior under beam-blockage gaps, and no evident center distortion at W = 15.
Table 3: Normalized-convolution algorithm flow for local statistics.
